[Theory: The claim "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is not correct.  
The claim is based on a misunderstanding of the physical properties of pecans and the mechanics of biting. Pecans are hard, but they are not strong enough to crack teeth. Teeth are made of enamel, which is strong but not as strong as the material of a pecan shell. Biting a pecan would cause the shell to crack, but it would not cause teeth to crack. The idea that teeth can be cracked by biting a pecan is a common misconception. This claim can be verified by human common sense without a web search, as it relies on basic knowledge of the properties of pecans and teeth.]
